title: new cassandra-driver Client with sslOptions.rejectUnauthorized: false — the Cassandra TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The Cassandra client validates the node certificate (sslOptions.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new cassandra-driver Client is created with sslOptions: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the Cassandra/ScyllaDB node's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — exposing every query, credential, and row. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the cluster CA via sslOptions.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No cassandra-driver Client construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The Cassandra Client does not set sslOptions.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: sslOptions: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a Cassandra/Scylla cluster with a self-signed cert and ships, disabling MITM protection on the database connection.
